items.title: Bring a mobile 3,840Wh+ Anker SOLIX F3800 power station to adventures while at an exclusive $1,899 price (Reg. $2,399)
items.summary:
While Anker’s SOLIX Spring Sale has ended, the brand is still offering some solid Power Deal savings on power stations, with no clue as to whether there will be any Easter Sale lineup as the holiday approaches in a few days – plus, these deals are all the better with our exclusive bonus 5% savings code on orders over $1,000. One such deal currently is Anker’s SOLIX F3800 Portable Power Station down at $1,899.05 shipped, after using the exclusive code 9TO5DEALS5 at checkout, beating its Amazon pricing by $101. You may see it listed with a $3,999 MSRP, but it more often can be found climbing as high as $2,399 these days at Amazon, which we previously saw at $1,899 in the Spring Sale that just ended ($1,804 with our code), while a 24-hour flash sale in February gave us our first sighting of the $1,799 low since Black Friday. All-in-all, the deal here is a solid $500 markdown ($2,100 off the MSRP) to the third-lowest price we have tracked. You can also find the upgraded Anker SOLIX F3800 Plus Portable Power Station at $2,374.05 shipped, using the exclusive code, which beats out Amazon by $125.

Best Buy is also matching Amazon’s pricing and is likewise offering a FREE $50 Best Buy e-gift card with purchase. items.summary:
Over at Woot, you’ll find Razer’s BlackShark V3 Pro gaming headset in white going for $189.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee will apply otherwise). This headset typically retails for $250, so you’re looking at a solid $60 price drop. While that may not sound like a huge price drop, it’s still a notable one considering this white variant has only seen a handful of discounts so far and had never dipped below the $220 mark until now. Today’s deal slices another $30 off that previous low, marking a new all-time low price. You’ll also find the 2XKO Edition of the headset — sporting white-and-purple styling inspired by the League of Legends character Ahri from the upcoming fighting game 2XKO — available at the same $189.99 rate. Meanwhile, Best Buy has the black model listed at $199.99 shipped and is tossing in a FREE $25 Taco Bell e-gift card, which arguably offers better overall value, though the Woot deal is still better if you’re looking for a straight-up cash discount.
